home.h1: Emotion, intention and social meaning in spoken language
home.p1: We investigate how people convey feelings, attitudes, and intentions when they speak: information carried not only by language itself (what is said), but also by the voice and facial expressions accompanying it (how it is said).
home.p2: The lab's overarching goal is to understand how adults express emotion and intention in spoken communication, how listeners decode these signals, and the brain mechanisms involved. We also study social and cultural factors that shape speech communication, combining acoustic, behavioural, EEG/ERP and fMRI methods to test theoretical hypotheses.
home.pi_role_extra: Vice Dean, Institute of Language Sciences, SISU
home.pi_bio:
Dr. Xiaoming Jiang is Professor, Doctoral Supervisor, and Vice Dean at the Institute of Language Sciences, Shanghai International Studies University. He works on psycholinguistics and neurolinguistics, with a focus on training the next generation of researchers in these fields. Since 2021 he has been continuously listed among Elsevier's Highly Cited Chinese Researchers in Foreign Languages & Literature. His research received the Best Paper Award in Speech Communication from ISCA and EURASIP, and he has been selected for Shanghai's Young Top-Notch Talent Program and the Chenguang Scholar Program.

The lab investigates how emotion, intention, and social meaning are conveyed in spoken communication — across vocal-emotion encoding and decoding, neuropragmatics and social communication, AI voice perception, cross-linguistic and cross-cultural cognition, and applied neurolinguistics with brain–machine-interface foundations. We combine acoustic, behavioural, eye-tracking, EEG/ERP, and fMRI methods to study multimodal information processing in both healthy and clinical populations.

The lab welcomes curious students, postdoctoral researchers, and visiting scholars interested in psycholinguistics and neurolinguistics, and we are open to collaborations with universities, research institutes, and clinical partners worldwide. Please get in touch by email.

people.intro: Current members, alumni, and our network of key collaborators worldwide. Click an avatar to expand a member's details.

projects.intro: Our research is organized around several interlocking themes, all centred on how vocal communication conveys social and emotional meaning.

facilities.intro: Our research is supported by access to multiple test sites at the Institute of Language Sciences, Shanghai International Studies University, including 7 sub-facilities (Speech, Eye-tracking, EEG, Behavioral, Child Language, Language Data Processing & Application, Smart Learning) under the university's Key Laboratory of Language Sciences and Multilingual Artificial Intelligence, plus access to a 3T MRI system at the university's Brain Imaging Center.

news.intro: Recent recruitment calls, collaboration opportunities, and academic activity announcements.

contact.intro: Email us about admissions, collaboration, or visits.
contact.admissions_body:
Ph.D. tracks

  1. Cognitive neuroscience of voice
  2. Speech-brain cognitive computing and AI

Master's students and undergraduate research assistants are also welcome. Please email Prof. Jiang with your CV and a brief research statement.
contact.intl_body:
• China Studies Program (CSP) Ph.D. Fellowship
  • Shanghai Sci-tech Co-research Program

International applicants are encouraged to email Prof. Jiang directly to discuss application materials, reference letters, and timing.
contact.collab_body:
Open to collaboration with universities, research institutes, and clinical partners worldwide:

  • Visiting positions
  • Joint projects
  • Co-supervision of students
  • Clinical collaboration
contact.visit_body:
Prof. Jiang's office is in Bldg. 5, Rm. 156, on the Songjiang campus of Shanghai International Studies University. From central Shanghai, take Metro Line 9 to Songjiang University Town station, then transfer to the campus shuttle (about 15 min).

Visitors from abroad are encouraged to contact us in advance; we can help arrange accommodation and visa documents.

teaching.intro: Standing undergraduate and graduate courses taught by Prof. Jiang.
teaching.psycho_body: Introduction to psycholinguistics at undergraduate and graduate levels — cognitive mechanisms of lexical access, sentence comprehension, and discourse / context processing, alongside the core experimental paradigms and methods (behavioural reaction time, eye-tracking, ERP).
teaching.neuro_body: Neurolinguistics with a focus on the brain mechanisms of language — language-relevant brain regions and networks, non-invasive neuroimaging methods (EEG / ERP and fMRI), and the neural bases of language function in healthy and clinical populations.
teaching.disorders_body: Topics in language disorders — patterns of language, voice, and pragmatic impairment in aphasia, specific language impairment, autism spectrum, Parkinson's disease, and related clinical populations, with assessment methods and translational applications.
